title: ulmus scabra subf. pendula
intro: Ulmus scabra subf. pendula, also known as the Japanese weeping elm, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the family Ulmaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Ulmus scabra subf. pendula include its ability to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce blood pressure. Herbal preparations of Ulmus scabra subf. pendula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
